title: [BUG] Cells from loaded files reuse the same style object
author: frosas

comments.author: josephaw1022
comments.body: # What exactly is different about the cell's styling with the file provided?

comments.author: pointlesscore
gudgyo
comments.body:
Any news on this? I'm having the same issue, cells getting modified on unrelated never touched sheets etc.
If you have some kind of workaround it would much appreciated as well, thanks!

comments.author: bradwoo8621
Echo-YeZi
comments.body:
> Having same issue, finally found why.
> 
> lib read styles from excel, and the impacted cells share one style object in excel file. so if update the style property, it impacts other cells. the solution is clone style and assign to cell again.
> 
> ```ts
> await workbook.xlsx.readFile("spreadsheet.xlsx");
> //...
> 
> // do not assign fill directly
> sheet.getCell('A1').fill = {
>   type: 'pattern',
>   pattern: 'darkTrellis', 
>   fgColor: { argb: 'FFFFFF00' },
>   bgColor: { argb: 'FF0000FF' },
> }
> 
> // change to this
> sheet.getCell('A1').style = {
>   ...(sheet.getCell('A1').style || {}),
>   fill: {
>     type: 'pattern',
>     pattern: 'darkTrellis', 
>     fgColor: { argb: 'FFFFFF00' },
>     bgColor: { argb: 'FF0000FF' }
>   }
> }
> 
> 
> // ...
> await workbook.xlsx.writeFile("spreadsheet.xlsx");
> ```
> 
> excel file is known as a zip file, simply change the ext file name, and extract. to find the worksheet and style xml, will found excel save style and shared by multiple cells if they are same.
> 
> So lib exactly follows the excel format, seems it is not an issue. And for #1994, seems caused by the same reason.

it works! thanks a lot

comments.author: julimuchi
comments.body: It works!! Thanks!! you save me!

comments.author: thswanson97
comments.body: This also saved me! This issue needs to be linked or the problem needs to be referenced in the style section of the readme docs. Such weird behavior.

comments.author: kang-int
comments.body:
> Having same issue, finally found why.
> 
> lib read styles from excel, and the impacted cells share one style object in excel file. so if update the style property, it impacts other cells. the solution is clone style and assign to cell again.
> 
> await workbook.xlsx.readFile("spreadsheet.xlsx");
> //...
> 
> // do not assign fill directly
> sheet.getCell('A1').fill = {
>   type: 'pattern',
>   pattern: 'darkTrellis', 
>   fgColor: { argb: 'FFFFFF00' },
>   bgColor: { argb: 'FF0000FF' },
> }
> 
> // change to this
> sheet.getCell('A1').style = {
>   ...(sheet.getCell('A1').style || {}),
>   fill: {
>     type: 'pattern',
>     pattern: 'darkTrellis', 
>     fgColor: { argb: 'FFFFFF00' },
>     bgColor: { argb: 'FF0000FF' }
>   }
> }
> 
> 
> // ...
> await workbook.xlsx.writeFile("spreadsheet.xlsx");
> excel file is known as a zip file, simply change the ext file name, and extract. to find the worksheet and style xml, will found excel save style and shared by multiple cells if they are same.
> 
> So lib exactly follows the excel format, seems it is not an issue. And for [#1994](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/issues/1994), seems caused by the same reason.

The author should add this solution to the doc!!!!! I wasted half a day on this "bug"
